questionHtml: Perform a test on the data from school A to show it is reasonable to assume a linear relationship between effort scores and improvements in IB points. You may assume effort scores follow a normal distribution.
markschemeHtml:
H 0 :  ρ = 0

H 0  : ρ > 0         A1

Note: Allow hypotheses to be expressed in words.

p -value = 0.00157        A1

(0.00157 < 0.01) there is a significant evidence of a (linear) correlation between effort and improvement (so it is reasonable to assume a linear relationship)        R1

[3 marks]

meta.title: Perform a test on the data from school A to show it is reasonable to assume a linear relationship between effort scores and improvements in IB points. You may assume effort scores
breadcrumbLabels: SL 4.11—Expected, observed, hypotheses, chi squared, gof, t-test

AHL 4.18—T and Z test, type I and II errors
